headline: Inference throughput and interence-speed breakthroughs dominate the cycle as recommended channels stress-test trillion-parameter and diffusion-based models past 1,000 tokens per second.
body: Better Stack benchmarks two distinct routes to extreme speed: Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model clears 1,000 tokens per second using selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [6], while Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applies image diffusion to text via uniform state diffusion and bidirectional multi-pass refinement, shifting work from memory-bound to compute-bound on H100s [8]. NVIDIA reinforces the diffusion thread by cutting denoising from fifty steps toward one through dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and student-teacher distillation for near real-time video on Blackwell B200 [13]. IBM frames the economics with smart AI routing that optimizes on cost and trust rather than defaulting to the largest model [4], and a separate strand tracks the claimed export-control removal of Claude Fable 5, covered by Cole Medin with pre-ban benchmarks against Opus 4.8 [7] and by Cyril Imhof on the EU access angle [9]. Agentic infrastructure also recurs: Kagenti applies identity and delegation chains for multi-agent security [0] and Omnigent orchestrates Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers with human-in-the-loop governance [1].
